reasoning: There is definitive evidence that massive protests occurred in Turkey on May 1, 2025, with 286,584 participants across 78 provinces (Reuters, Al-Monitor). This easily exceeds the 10,000 participant threshold. Turkey has been in a sustained protest period since March 2025 following the arrest of Istanbul Mayor Imamoglu, with hundreds of thousands attending demonstrations and weekly protests planned. The Global Protest Tracker is designed to track "significant antigovernment protests" (Carnegie methodology), and May Day protests with nearly 300,000 participants in 78 provinces would qualify as "significant." Turkey is also listed in the question background with a Wikipedia link to "2025 Turkish protests," suggesting awareness of recent protest activity. Given the scale, official government acknowledgment, and sustained protest momentum, there's a very high likelihood (~75%) these protests will appear in the Tracker or similar protests will occur before July 1, 2025.
